call"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":456,"created_at":"2017-12-08T19:10:10Z","updated_at":"2024-04-04T19:28:09Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/24990","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/24990","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/24990.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/24990.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This PR addresses #554, #5571, and #22710 by "currying" underscores in function calls like `f(_,y)` into anonymous function expressions `x -> f(x,y)`.  (Note that `_.foo` works and turns into `x -> x.foo` since it is equivalent to a `getfield` call, and `_[i]` works and turns into `x -> x[i]`, since it is equivalent to a `getindex(_,i)` call.)

This will help us get rid of functions like `equalto` (#23812) or `occursin` (#24967), is useful for "destructuring" as discussed in #22710, and should generally be convenient in lots of cases to avoid having to explicitly do `x -> f(x,y)`.

Some simplifying design decisions that I made:

* The currying is "tight", i.e. it only converts the immediately surrounding function call into a lambda, [as suggested by](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/22710#issuecomment-314259250) @JeffBezanson (and [as in Scala](https://www.scala-lang.org/files/archive/spec/2.11/06-expressions.html#placeholder-syntax-for-anonymous-functions)).   So, e.g. `f(g(_,y))` is equivalent to `f(x -> g(x,y))`.   (Note that something like `find(!(_ in c), y)` will work fine, because the `!` operator works on functions; you can also use `_ ∉ c`.)  Any other rule seems hard to make comprehensible and consistent.

* ~~Only a single underscore is allowed.  `f(_,_)` throws an error: this case seems ambiguous to me (do you want `x -> f(x,x)` or `x,y -> f(x,y)`?), so it seemed better to punt on this for now.  We can always add a meaning for multiple underscores later.~~ Similar to Scala, multiple underscores are converted into multiple arguments in the order they appear.  e.g. `f(_,y,_)` is equivalent to `(x,z) -> f(x,y,z)`.  See [rationale below](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/24990#issuecomment-350879561).

The implementation is pretty trivial.  If people are in favor, I will add
- [x] Tests
- [x] Documentation
- [ ] Fix interaction with broadcasting `f.(x, _)`
- [ ] Fix interaction with keyword arguments `f(x; y=_)`
- [ ] Support chained comparisons, e.g. `3 ≤ _ ≤ 10`, since they parse as a single expression?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/24990/reactions","total_count":300,"+1":178,"-1":4,"laugh":0,"hooray":13,"confused":2,"heart":87,"rocket":10,"eyes":6},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/24990/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2038051987","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/24990#issuecomment-2038051987","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/24990","id":2038051987,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM55ejST","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-04-04T19:28:08Z","updated_at":"2024-04-04T19:28:08Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Imho the advantage of being statically understandable probably outweighs the flexibility gained from doing that. PR adds support for parsing `.a` as `x->x.a`. This kind of thing has come up multiple times in the past, but I'm currently finding myself doing a lot of work on nested structs where this operation is very common.

In general, we've had the position that this kind of thing should be a special case of the short-currying syntax (e.g. #38713), but I actually think that might be a false constraint. In particular, `.a` is a bit of a worst case for the curry syntax. If there is no requirement for `.a` to be excessively short in an eventual underscore curry syntax, I think that could open more options.

That said, any syntax proposal of course needs to stand on its own, so let me motivate the cases where I think this plays:

# Motivation 
## Curried getfield

I think this is probably the most obvious and often requested. The syntax here is very useful for situations where higher order functions operate on collections of records:

1. `map(.a, vec)` and reductions for getting the fields of an object - also includes things like `sum(.price, items)`
2. Predicates like `sort(vecs, by=.x)` or `filter(!(.deleted), entries)`
3. In pipelines `vecs |> .x |> sqrt |> sum`

I think that's mostly what people are thinking of, but the use case for this syntax is more general.

## A syntax for lenses

Packages like Accessors.jl provide lens-like abstractions. Currently these are written as `lens = @optic _.a`. An example use of Accessors.jl is (from their documentation)
```
julia> modify(lowercase, (;a="AA", b="BB"), @optic _.a)
T("aa", "BB")
```

This PR can be thought of as providing lenses first class syntax, as in:
```
julia> modify(lowercase, (;a="AA", b="BB"), .a)
T("aa", "BB")
```

## Symbol index generalization to hierarchical structures

We have a lot of packages in the ecosystem that support named axes of various forms (Canonical examples might be DataFrames and NamedArrays, but there's probably two dozen of these). Generally the way that this syntax works is that people use quoted symbols for indexing:

```
df[5, :col]
```

However, this breaks down when there is hierachical composition involved. For example, for simulation models, you often build parameter sets and solutions out of hierarchies of simpler models.

There's a couple of solutions that people have come up with for this problem:
1. Some packages parse out hierachy from symbol names: `sol[:var"my.nested.hierachy.state"]`
2. Other packages have a global root object: `sol[○.my.nested.hierarchy.state]` 2a. A variant of this is using the object as its own root `sol[sol.my.nested.hierarchy.state]` 2b. Yet another variant is having the root object be context specific `sol[sys.my.nested.hierarchy.state]`
3. Yet other packages put symbolic names into the global namespaces `sol[my.nested.hierarchy.state]`

These solutions are all lacking. 1 requires string manipulation for composition, the various variants of 2 are ok, but there is no agreement among packages what the root object looks like or is spelled, and even so, it's an extra export and 3 pollutes the global namespaces.

By using the same mechanism here, we essentially standardize the solution `2`, but make the root object implicit.`

# Additional considerations

- As mentioned above, this was previously argued to be a special case of underscore currying. Esp if used for the other use cases above (which don't necessarily use this applicatively), I don't think that's necessarily dispositive, but as always it's better to add one syntax rather than two.

- This is of course also related to the long-standing (almost 10 years - oh no) issue of "mutating immutables" (ref #11902). I think this makes sense independent of any proposal there, but it's worth thinking about.

- Every once in a while people request broadcasted getproperty along the lines of `vec..a`. This of course provides that with `(.a).(vec)`. I had briefly explored not requiring the parentheses, along the lines of `.a.(vec)`, but it didn't feel right when I played with it, so this PR explicitly reserves that syntax.

!!! note
This only provides an flisp implementation for people to play around with. As such, this needs `JULIA_USE_FLISP_PARSER=true`. I will provide a JuliaSyntax implementation once there's consensus on ```jl

97

help?> Int
search: Int Int8 Int64 Int32 Int16 Int128 Integer intersect intersect! InteractiveUtils InterruptException

  Int64 <: Signed


  64-bit signed integer type.

help?> Char
search: Char ischardev Cchar skipchars Cuchar Cwchar_t AbstractChar catch_backtrace Channel eachmatch AbstractChannel

  Char(c::Union{Number,AbstractChar})


  Char is a 32-bit AbstractChar type that is the default representation of characters in Julia. Char is the type
  used for character literals like 'x' and it is also the element type of String.

  In order to losslessly represent arbitrary byte streams stored in a String, a Char value may store information
  that cannot be converted to a Unicode codepoint — converting such a Char to UInt32 will throw an error. The
  isvalid(c::Char) function can be used to query whether c represents a valid Unicode character.

Possibly, also write `mean?(xs)` to automatically handle inputs with missing values.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36628/reactions","total_count":9,"+1":5,"-1":4,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36628/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2045898055","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36628#issuecomment-2045898055","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36628","id":2045898055,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM558e1H","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-04-09T19:14:40Z","updated_at":"2024-04-09T19:14:40Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Imho `Union{T, Nothing}` is one of those often too-convenient features that lures people into unsafe usage where they have no proof ruling out `Nothing <: T` and hence they accidentally merge the failure case with the success-with-value-`nothing` case. 

Eg if `findfirst` is used inside a library where `T` is generic and set by the (external) user the library will do the wrong thing.
```jl
julia> findfirst(==(1), Dict(nothing=>10, 2=>20))
# incorrectly assume it wasn't found
```

Using `Some` to distinguish success from failure is preferable for generic behavior.


This issue is discussed more in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/29565 and by Takafumi in this very thread above. https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36628#issuecomment-659146056","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2045898055/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

PR adds support for parsing `.a` as `x->x.a`. This kind of thing has come up multiple times in the past, but I'm currently finding myself doing a lot of work on nested structs where this operation is very common.

In general, we've had the position that this kind of thing should be a special case of the short-currying syntax (e.g. #38713), but I actually think that might be a false constraint. In particular, `.a` is a bit of a worst case for the curry syntax. If there is no requirement for `.a` to be excessively short in an eventual underscore curry syntax, I think that could open more options.

That said, any syntax proposal of course needs to stand on its own, so let me motivate the cases where I think this plays:

# Motivation 
## Curried getfield

I think this is probably the most obvious and often requested. The syntax here is very useful for situations where higher order functions operate on collections of records:

1. `map(.a, vec)` and reductions for getting the fields of an object - also includes things like `sum(.price, items)`
2. Predicates like `sort(vecs, by=.x)` or `filter(!(.deleted), entries)`
3. In pipelines `vecs |> .x |> sqrt |> sum`

I think that's mostly what people are thinking of, but the use case for this syntax is more general.

## A syntax for lenses

Packages like Accessors.jl provide lens-like abstractions. Currently these are written as `lens = @optic _.a`. An example use of Accessors.jl is (from their documentation)
```
julia> modify(lowercase, (;a="AA", b="BB"), @optic _.a)
T("aa", "BB")
```

This PR can be thought of as providing lenses first class syntax, as in:
```
julia> modify(lowercase, (;a="AA", b="BB"), .a)
T("aa", "BB")
```

## Symbol index generalization to hierarchical structures

We have a lot of packages in the ecosystem that support named axes of various forms (Canonical examples might be DataFrames and NamedArrays, but there's probably two dozen of these). Generally the way that this syntax works is that people use quoted symbols for indexing:

```
df[5, :col]
```

However, this breaks down when there is hierachical composition involved. For example, for simulation models, you often build parameter sets and solutions out of hierarchies of simpler models.

There's a couple of solutions that people have come up with for this problem:
1. Some packages parse out hierachy from symbol names: `sol[:var"my.nested.hierachy.state"]`
2. Other packages have a global root object: `sol[○.my.nested.hierarchy.state]` 2a. A variant of this is using the object as its own root `sol[sol.my.nested.hierarchy.state]` 2b. Yet another variant is having the root object be context specific `sol[sys.my.nested.hierarchy.state]`
3. Yet other packages put symbolic names into the global namespaces `sol[my.nested.hierarchy.state]`

These solutions are all lacking. 1 requires string manipulation for composition, the various variants of 2 are ok, but there is no agreement among packages what the root object looks like or is spelled, and even so, it's an extra export and 3 pollutes the global namespaces.

By using the same mechanism here, we essentially standardize the solution `2`, but make the root object implicit.`

# Additional considerations

- As mentioned above, this was previously argued to be a special case of underscore currying. Esp if used for the other use cases above (which don't necessarily use this applicatively), I don't think that's necessarily dispositive, but as always it's better to add one syntax rather than two.

- This is of course also related to the long-standing (almost 10 years - oh no) issue of "mutating immutables" (ref #11902). I think this makes sense independent of any proposal there, but it's worth thinking about.

- Every once in a while people request broadcasted getproperty along the lines of `vec..a`. This of course provides that with `(.a).(vec)`. I had briefly explored not requiring the parentheses, along the lines of `.a.(vec)`, but it didn't feel right when I played with it, so this PR explicitly reserves that syntax.

!!! note
This only provides an flisp implementation for people to play around with. As such, this needs `JULIA_USE_FLISP_PARSER=true`. I will provide a JuliaSyntax implementation once there's consensus on semantics.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53946/reactions","total_count":5,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":5,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53946/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2045976619","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53946#issuecomment-2045976619","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53946","id":2045976619,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM558yAr","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-04-09T20:13:50Z","updated_at":"2024-04-09T20:13:50Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Imho the original underscore issue has ~three different proposals which are all pretty good and more general than `.a` while only taking one extra character. Some ideas
- Use `∈` instead of `in`. I like it, I'm sure not everyone will. It's annoying to type on websites.
- Use the Haskell syntax where backticks make a function into an infix operator

```
> f x y = x + y
> f 1 2
3
> 1 `f` 2
3
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54106/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54106/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

- Use `∈` instead of `in`. I like it, I'm sure not everyone will. It's annoying to type on websites.
- Use the Haskell syntax where backticks make a function into an infix operator

```haskell
> f x y = x + y
> f 1 2
3
> 1 `f` 2
3
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54106/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":1,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54106/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2059918113","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54106#issuecomment-2059918113","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54106","id":2059918113,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM56x9sh","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-04-16T21:02:35Z","updated_at":"2024-04-16T21:02:35Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Oh right, `isa` is the other one. ```jl
julia> function foo(x) x end
foo (generic function with 3 methods)

function foo(x) (x) end
#              ╙ ── whitespace is not allowed here
```

encodings"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":11,"created_at":"2024-04-22T13:50:28Z","updated_at":"2024-04-22T19:34:19Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I don't love that we have our own custom mapping from strings to unicode characters. It is not friendly to users who switch between Julia and other programs that define similar though not identical mappings.

Given that we _do_ have this mapping, I think we should
- [ ] 1. Continue to add common characters to it as requested (and document some guidelines for what characters to support)
- [ ] 2. Support all Unicode symbols by official name (e.g. `\MUSICAL_SYMBOL_SINGLE_BARLINE`)
- [ ] 3. Try to find and adopt a canonical mapping from strings to characters that is more ergonomic than unicode (e.g. latex?)

#54156 
Changes part of #53896

The main difference between `view` and `unsafe_vector` is that the result of `view` has a fixed size, and creates a copy when passed to `String`.

The `String(view(` pattern is fairly common: https://juliahub.com/ui/Search?q=String(view&type=code and as more packages start using `Memory` it may be hard to tell if `view` is being used safely or not.

I'm not sure about the name `unsafe_vector`, but the docstring says 

> It is only safe to
    resize `v` if `m` is subseqently not used.

So I added an `unsafe_` prefix.

Ref https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53552#issuecomment-2024288283 for a discussion on why `view` on a `Memory` should produce an `Array`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54273/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54273/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2080163008","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54273#issuecomment-2080163008","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54273","id":2080163008,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM57_MTA","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-04-26T21:49:45Z","updated_at":"2024-04-26T21:49:45Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Triage named it `view` in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53552.

#54156 
Changes part of #53896

The main difference between `view` and `unsafe_vector` is that the result of `view` has a fixed size, and creates a copy when passed to `String`.

The `String(view(` pattern is fairly common: https://juliahub.com/ui/Search?q=String(view&type=code and as more packages start using `Memory` it may be hard to tell if `view` is being used safely or not.

I'm not sure about the name `unsafe_vector`, but the docstring says 

> It is only safe to
    resize `v` if `m` is subseqently not used.

So I added an `unsafe_` prefix.

Ref https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53552#issuecomment-2024288283 for a discussion on why `view` on a `Memory` should produce an `Array`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54273/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54273/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2081939386","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/54273#issuecomment-2081939386","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54273","id":2081939386,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM58F9-6","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-04-29T05:55:59Z","updated_at":"2024-04-29T05:55:59Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":""Base.wrap" doesn't seem like it means anything in particular, it's too uninformative. {"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53552","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53552/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53552/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53552/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53552","id":2164663736,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6BBiW4","number":53552,"title":"`wrap` is a very generic name to export","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-03-02T06:59:31Z","updated_at":"2024-03-02T06:59:31Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Apologies in advance for the bikeshedding.

`wrap` was introduced as the safe alternative to `unsafe_wrap` in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/52049 in December by @MasonProtter. 

Maybe this is the right call but I want to at least flag the issue. `wrap` a very a generic name and there are many packages using it for all kinds of purposes, and many others would likely want to choose that name in the future. https://github.com/search?q=%22function+wrap%22+language%3Ajulia&type=code

Options:
- No change. Users will qualify `Package.wrap` instead of `using` packages. Many people who would write a function `wrap` will probably decide to choose a different name for their function.
- Don't export `wrap`. Users will write `Base.wrap` instead of `wrap`, which could be annoying if it's in common usage.
- Rename it. `wrap_memory`? `Array`? `array`? something else?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53552/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53552/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

> 
> 
>   Like reduce, but with guaranteed left associativity. If provided, the keyword argument init will be used exactly
>   once. In general, it will be necessary to provide init to work with empty collections.

I don't think it's clear from this whether `op` will receive `(accumulator, x)` or `(x, accumulator)`. The answer is `(accumulator, x)` which is necessary in order to support asymmetric `op`s like `push!`:

```jl
julia> foldl(push!, 1:2; init=Int[])
2-element Vector{Int64}:
 1
 2
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53562/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53562/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53678","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53678/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53678/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53678/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53678","id":2177240522,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6Bxg3K","number":53678,"title":"What is used for value display in REPL?","user":{"login":"aplavin","id":687995,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY4Nzk5NQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/687995?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aplavin","html_url":"https://github.com/aplavin","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aplavin/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aplavin/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aplavin/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aplavin/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aplavin/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aplavin/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aplavin/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aplavin/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aplavin/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-03-09T14:12:58Z","updated_at":"2024-03-11T02:06:27Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"My original question is "which function is used by the REPL to display values", and I couldn't really find a definite answer.

Docs point to `display(x)`
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/6f8ba49e157d4b0de3726be8613a6ae607c5ccd2/doc/src/manual/types.md?plain=1#L1483-L1484

and to `show`

https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/6f8ba49e157d4b0de3726be8613a6ae607c5ccd2/stdlib/REPL/docs/src/index.md?plain=1#L60

on different pages.

But this is definitely not the whole story, the output of `display(x)` and just `x` in REPL is different:
```julia
julia> using StructArrays, JLArrays  # or any actual GPU array instead of JLArray

julia> x = StructArray(a=JLArray(rand(3)))
3-element StructArray(::JLArray{Float64, 1}) with eltype @NamedTuple{a::Float64}:
 (a = 0.7284120253405527,)
 (a = 0.12606390581511362,)
 (a = 0.6120157378655939,)

julia> x |> display
3-element StructArray(::JLArray{Float64, 1}) with eltype @NamedTuple{a::Float64}:
ERROR: Scalar indexing is disallowed.
...
```

So, would be nice to document how it actually works... I personally don't understand it for now.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53678/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53678/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1987501930","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53678#issuecomment-1987501930","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53678","id":1987501930,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM52dt9q","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-11T02:06:26Z","updated_at":"2024-03-11T02:06:26Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"See also

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53717","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53717/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53717/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53717/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53717","id":2182551983,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6CFxmv","number":53717,"title":"2.0: Make `propertynames(x)` return `()` by default","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-03-12T19:48:18Z","updated_at":"2024-03-12T19:48:18Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The docstring of `propertynames` says

>   propertynames(x) may return only "public" property names that are part of the documented interface of x. If you   want it to also return "private" property names intended for internal use, pass true for the optional second   argument. REPL tab completion on x. shows only the private=false properties.

This distinction between public and private properties is a good one, but the distinction in `propertynames` isn't very useful if it returns all by default, because the caller can't rely on all the returned properties being public. 


Alternatively in 1.x `publicproperties(x)` could be introduced.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53717/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53717/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

> In all languages I am familiar with, assertion checks are for debugging, and can be disabled in "release builds".

However, Rust is a counterexample: it distinguishes [`assert`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/macro.assert.html) and [`debug_assert`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/macro.debug_assert.html):

> Assertions are always checked in both debug and release builds, and cannot be disabled. See [debug_assert!](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/macro.debug_assert.html) for assertions that are not enabled in release builds by default.

I sorta think consistency with Rust is almost as good as consistency with a bunch of other languages. There is another strong argument in favor, which is that a fair amount code already acts as if `@assert` is nonremovable. 

Are there any other arguments against merging? ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2014066688/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

 :a

- `vcat` puts string arguments in a vector: `vcat("ab", "cd") == ["ab", "cd"]`, whereas I want `append("ab", "cd") == "abcd"`.

want a non-mutating version of `append!` that puts two sequences together.


```jl
julia> (append(x::T, y::T) where T<: AbstractVector) = append!(copy(x), y);

julia> (append(x::T, y::T) where T<:AbstractString)= join([x, y]);

julia> append([:a, :b], [:c, :d])
4-element Vector{Symbol}:
 :a
 :b
 :c
 :d

julia> append("ab", "cd")
"abcd"
```

One idea I like is to define some operators without precedence, so that any ambiguity can be resolved by parentheses. It's obvious to the reader what is happening, and there is no need for different users to argue about what the precedence should be. Moreover, in the common case where only one operator call is used, there is no need for parentheses at all.\r\n\r\n","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53268/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53268/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

sets"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":9,"created_at":"2024-01-24T19:28:45Z","updated_at":"2024-02-10T22:26:46Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I want a non-mutating version of `append!` that puts two sequences together.


```jl
julia> (append(x::T, y::T) where T<: AbstractVector) = append!(copy(x), y);

julia> (append(x::T, y::T) where T<:AbstractString)= join([x, y]);

julia> append([:a, :b], [:c, :d])
4-element Vector{Symbol}:
 :a
 :b
 :c
 :d

julia> append("ab", "cd")
"abcd"
```


When the argument types differ we could have some promotion logic.


Here are the properties implied by the "sequence" requirement. They should hold unless one of the functions on the left hand side throws an error.


- `append(eachindex(a), eachindex(b)) == eachindex(append(a,b))`.
- `((x in a) || (x in b)) iff (x in append(a,b))`
- `i isa Integer implies a[i] == append(a,b)[i]`
- `i isa Integer implies b[i] == append(a,b)[length(a)+i]`

I think that means for `Matrix` arguments it's `hcat` and for `Set` it's `union`, for `Tuple` it does the obvious thing. For `NamedTuple` it can be defined just like `Tuple` unless the `any(map(==, keys(a), keys(b)))`, in which case it can error or last wins (I prefer the error, probably). I'm not sure what to say about `Dict` and `Dictionary`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53040/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53040/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1937289689","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53040#issuecomment-1937289689","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53040","id":1937289689,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5zeLHZ","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-02-10T22:26:45Z","updated_at":"2024-02-10T22:26:45Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"@mcabbott Edited OP.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1937289689/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

6-element Vector{Int64}:
 1
 2
 1
 2
 1
 2

julia> collect(Iterators.repeated([1,2], 3))
3-element Vector{Vector{Int64}}:
 [1, 2]
 [1, 2]
 [1, 2]
```

Merge when all tests are passing"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2022-10-27T18:44:23Z","updated_at":"2024-02-12T04:14:32Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/47354","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/47354","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/47354.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/47354.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"At present `Iterators.repeated(iter, n)` is the lazy cousin of `fill(iter, n)`, but there is no iterator for `repeat(iter, n)`. This PR proposes that `Iterators.cycle(iter, n)` should fill that role.

This relates to the one-argument form in the same way as `Iterators.repeated`. That is, `cycle(iter)` means `cycle(iter, Inf)` in the same way that `repeated(iter)` means `repeated(iter, Inf)`... or would be if Inf were an integer.

The implementation uses `flatten(repeated(xs, n))`. It could instead use `take(cycle(xs), n * length(xs))` but that only works when the contents has known length. `take(cycle...)` tends to be faster, perhaps it should be used when possible? Some timing below. But perhaps this detail is a secondary question.

<details>

```julia
julia> takecycle(x, n::Int) = Iterators.take(Iterators.cycle(x), n * length(x));

julia> flatrep(x, n::Int) = Iterators.flatten(Iterators.repeated(x, n));  # as in PR, first commit

julia> takecycle(1:10, 100) |> length
1000

julia> flatrep(1:10, 100) |> Base.haslength  # and won't be helped by 47353
false

julia> @btime collect(takecycle(1:10, 100));
  min 1.642 μs, mean 2.554 μs (1 allocation, 7.94 KiB)

julia> @btime collect(flatrep(1:10, 100));
  min 6.617 μs, mean 9.107 μs (6 allocations, 21.86 KiB)

julia> flatrep(Tuple(1:10), 100) |> Base.haslength  # behaves better with tuples, but not faster:
true

julia> @btime collect(takecycle($(Tuple(rand(10))), 100));
  min 1.100 μs, mean 1.977 μs (1 allocation, 7.94 KiB)

julia> @btime collect(flatrep($(Tuple(rand(10))), 100));
  min 10.458 μs, mean 11.220 μs (1 allocation, 7.94 KiB)
```

</details>","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47354/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47354/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1938043875","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/47354#issuecomment-1938043875","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47354","id":1938043875,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5zhDPj","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-02-12T04:14:31Z","updated_at":"2024-02-12T04:14:31Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"`map` is to `Iterators.map` as `repeat` is to `Iterators.repeat`? {"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53324","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53324/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53324/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53324/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53324","id":2133210793,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5_Jjap","number":53324,"title":"Combinator operators","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-02-13T22:12:28Z","updated_at":"2024-02-13T22:12:28Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I use the  `∘` composition operator often. There are two simplest generalizations of that operator to more data arguments `xs`: 

```jl
"g takes multiple arguments; f takes one."
(xs...) -> f(g(xs...))
"g takes one argument; f takes multiple."
(xs...) -> f((g(x) for x in xs)...)
```

For example, to find the max of the absolute value of each argument, the syntax would be 

```jl
⊚(f,g) = (xs...) -> f((g(x) for x in xs)...); # g takes one argument; f takes multiple.
∨(x...) = max(x...); # Generalization of logical_or.
↑(x) = abs(x)

-1 ∨⊚↑ -2 # 2
```

Currently we have:

```jl
julia> ((∨)⊚↑)(-1, -2)
2
```

But there are problems:

1.  `(∨)` requires parentheses, which is annoying. This could be solved by adding a precedence rule for designated combinator operators like `⊚`.

2. Infix doesn't work: `-1 (∨)⊚↑ -2` is a `ParseError`. This could be solved by letting combinator expressions be infix.

3. Prefix `((∨)⊚↑)` is wrapped in parentheses. This seems least likely to change, but not so bad.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53324/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53324/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

```jl
julia> using Dates, Accessors

julia> d = Date(2024, 12, 31)
2024-12-31

julia> @set month(d) = 1
2024-01-31

julia> t = DateTime(2024, 12, 31, 23, 59, 59)
2024-12-31T23:59:59

julia> @set minute(t) = 4
2024-12-31T23:04:59
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1942883845/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

methods.","user":{"login":"Tokazama","id":13888485,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEzODg4NDg1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13888485?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama","html_url":"https://github.com/Tokazama","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"
Her I've implemented what I think are some of the more straightforward non-mutating counterparts to mutating methods.
I borrowed liberally from @tkf's PR #33495 to get `Base.setindex` working here.
Should also resolve type piracy in ArrayInterface on `Base.setindex` https://github.com/JuliaArrays/ArrayInterface.jl/pull/305.
Also implemented `delete`, `deleteat`, and `insert` borrowing from work from ArrayInterface.
I also took a bit from #24836, but I thought`insert` was a more clear method name that can be used to accomplish what the non-mutating `push`/`pushfirst` methods (and a bit more).
I think some issues with functional collections can could really benefit from some basic tools like this (#34478, https://discourse.julialang.org/t/functional-implementation-of-collect/15177).
Currently `delete`, `deleteat`, and `insert` are in the export list but if that's too intrusive I can change methods.","user":{"login":"Tokazama","id":13888485,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEzODg4NDg1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13888485?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama","html_url":"https://github.com/Tokazama","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Tokazama/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"
Her I've implemented what I think are some of the more straightforward non-mutating counterparts to mutating methods.
I borrowed liberally from @tkf's PR #33495 to get `Base.setindex` working here.
Should also resolve type piracy in ArrayInterface on `Base.setindex` https://github.com/JuliaArrays/ArrayInterface.jl/pull/305.
Also implemented `delete`, `deleteat`, and `insert` borrowing from work from ArrayInterface.
I also took a bit from #24836, but I thought`insert` was a more clear method name that can be used to accomplish what the non-mutating `push`/`pushfirst` methods (and a bit more).
I think some issues with functional collections can could really benefit from some basic tools like this (#34478, https://discourse.julialang.org/t/functional-implementation-of-collect/15177).
Currently `delete`, `deleteat`, and `insert` are in the export list but if that's too intrusive I can change requests"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":7,"created_at":"2024-02-20T13:42:31Z","updated_at":"2024-02-25T09:14:59Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/53404","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53404","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53404.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53404.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This PR adds a new flag to control when a "julia specific" debug mode is active. When the debug mode is active the following happens:
- The `@assert` macro is active
- The `isdebug` function returns `true`.

Currently, the `--debug` has three options:
- `"yes"`: debug mode is active in scripts and packages
- `"script"`: debug mode is active in scripts (default)
- `"no"`: debug is not active anywhere

The debug mode is a "compile time option" meaning that it will recompile packages if it changes.

There are still many open questions regarding this:

- Should this instead use `-g` and coexist with the current behavior of `-g` or should it just steal it?
- Are the defaults here reasonable?
- When running `Pkg.test` should assertions be enabled everywhere or only in the tested package etc?
- Will it be expected that people run their tests under both debug and normal mode?
- Should `--debug` has any influence on `--check-bounds`?
- Probably many more

Subsumes https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/37874

Ref https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/41342, https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53223","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53404/reactions","total_count":7,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":7,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53404/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1962866821","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/53404#issuecomment-1962866821","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53404","id":1962866821,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM50_viF","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-02-25T09:14:58Z","updated_at":"2024-02-25T09:14:58Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I'm wondering if it would make sense to make it changeable at runtime. For example if I set up a function with a bunch of `@assert`s in it, call it a few times with test values for sanity checks with asserts enabled, and then disable assertions before passing it into a numerical optimizer where performance matters.

```jl
function f(x)
  @assert properties(x)
  sum(square, x)
end
@test f([0]) == 0
disabling_asserts() do 
  Optim.optimize(f, ...)
end
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1962866821/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

For example `names(Main; all=true, imported=true)` doesn't return `@chain`.

```jl
julia> using Chain

julia> names(Chain)
2-element Vector{Symbol}:
 Symbol("@chain")
 :Chain

julia> names((@__MODULE__); all=true, imported=true)
8-element Vector{Symbol}:
 Symbol("##meta#58")
 :Base
 :Chain
 :Core
 :InteractiveUtils
 :Main
 :eval
 :include
```

I would like a way to get this list.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53483/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53483/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

was introduced in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/52139 (https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/commit/42094740f8f48213c07d9a807d85f5d24cf6c27f), which hasn't made it into a release branch yet.

The term "documented" can have multiple meanings:
1. "X is documented" can mean "X is listed in the manual".
2. "X is documented" can mean "X has a docstring".

Therefore, I think the name `Docs.hasdoc` is a little confusing/ambiguous. I think it would be better to rename the function to `Docs.hasdocstring` (or `Docs.has_docstring`), to make it more clear what this function is doing.

Some more discussion in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/52139#issuecomment-1874748877 and is a tracking issue for public/exported symbols missing docstrings that were identified with the help of #52723.  Please check them off as they get fixed and link the corresponding PRs (which should also enable a `@test Docs.undocumented_names(ThisModule) == []` regression test).   

`Base` and public/exported submodules thereof:
- [ ] `Base`: `BufferStream`, `CanonicalIndexError`, `CapturedException`, `Filesystem`, `IOServer`, `InvalidStateException`, `Order`, `PipeEndpoint`, `Sort`, `TTY`
- [ ] `Base.Sys`: `CPU_NAME`, `JIT`, `cpu_info`, `cpu_summary`
- [ ] `Base.Filesystem`: `File`, `Filesystem`, `JL_O_APPEND`, `JL_O_ASYNC`, `JL_O_CLOEXEC`, `JL_O_CREAT`, `JL_O_DIRECT`, `JL_O_DIRECTORY`, `JL_O_DSYNC`, `JL_O_EXCL`, `JL_O_FSYNC`, `JL_O_LARGEFILE`, `JL_O_NDELAY`, `JL_O_NOATIME`, `JL_O_NOCTTY`, `JL_O_NOFOLLOW`, `JL_O_NONBLOCK`, `JL_O_PATH`, `JL_O_RANDOM`, `JL_O_RDONLY`, `JL_O_RDWR`, `JL_O_RSYNC`, `JL_O_SEQUENTIAL`, `JL_O_SHORT_LIVED`, `JL_O_SYNC`, `JL_O_TEMPORARY`, `JL_O_TMPFILE`, `JL_O_TRUNC`, `JL_O_WRONLY`, `S_IRGRP`, `S_IROTH`, `S_IRUSR`, `S_IRWXG`, `S_IRWXO`, `S_IRWXU`, `S_IWGRP`, `S_IWOTH`, `S_IWUSR`, `S_IXGRP`, `S_IXOTH`, `S_IXUSR`, `cptree`, `futime`, `rename`, `sendfile`, `unlink`

Standard Library:
- [ ] Libdl: `Libdl`
- [ ] TOML: `TOML`
- [ ] Artifacts: `Artifacts`
- [ ] Dates: `adjust`
- [ ] Unicode: `Unicode`
- [ ] Markdown: `html`, `latex`
- [ ] Serialization: `AbstractSerializer`, `Serializer`
- [ ] REPL: `AbstractREPL`, `BasicREPL`, `LineEditREPL`, `StreamREPL`
- [ ] Printf: `Printf`
- [ ] InteractiveUtils: `InteractiveUtils`
- [ ] Profile: `Allocs`
- [ ] LinearAlgebra: `ColumnNorm`, `LAPACKException`, `NoPivot`, `RankDeficientException`, `RowMaximum`, `RowNonZero`, `copy_transpose!`
- [ ] Logging: `AboveMaxLevel`, `BelowMinLevel`
- [ ] FileWatching: `FDWatcher`, `FileMonitor`, `FolderMonitor`, `PollingFileWatcher`
- [ ] SparseArrays: `fkeep!`
- [ ] Tar: `Tar`
- [ ] Pkg: `PKGMODE_MANIFEST`, `PKGMODE_PROJECT`, `PRESERVE_ALL`, `PRESERVE_ALL_INSTALLED`, `PRESERVE_DIRECT`, `PRESERVE_NONE`, `PRESERVE_SEMVER`, `PRESERVE_TIERED`, `PRESERVE_TIERED_INSTALLED`, `Pkg`, `PreserveLevel`, `Registry`, `UPLEVEL_MAJOR`, `UPLEVEL_MINOR`, `UPLEVEL_PATCH`","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52725/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52725/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1876153479","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52725#issuecomment-1876153479","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52725","id":1876153479,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5v09SH","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-01-04T00:34:39Z","updated_at":"2024-01-04T00:34:39Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Also for all modules `M` https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52581

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52747","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52747/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52747/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52747/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52747","id":2066247248,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM57KG5Q","number":52747,"title":"Public API for `Docs.doc`","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-01-04T19:52:26Z","updated_at":"2024-01-04T19:52:26Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"```
help?> Docs.doc
  Docs.doc(binding, sig)


  Return all documentation that matches both binding and sig.

  If getdoc returns a non-nothing result on the value of the binding, then a dynamic
  docstring is returned instead of one based on the binding itself.
```

It's useful to have a way to get the doc of a symbol in a module. `doc` is exported but can't be called because the first argument is a `Docs.Binding` which isn't public. 

One possibility is to create another method `doc(module, symbol)`. {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52704","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52704/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52704/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52704/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52704","id":2062808281,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM568_TZ","number":52704,"title":"Feature request: Get encoding of `AbstractString`","user":{"login":"jakobnissen","id":23193950,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIzMTkzOTUw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/23193950?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen","html_url":"https://github.com/jakobnissen","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jakobnissen/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-01-02T18:45:56Z","updated_at":"2024-01-04T21:01:08Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I do a lot with high-performance programming with strings. When you do that, it's often more efficient to work on the underlying bytes. Luckily, Julia enables that with `codeunits`.

However, there is no way of knowing generically, given some `AbstractString`, what encoding it uses - i.e, what the result of `codeunits` _means_. That makes it difficult (impossible?) to write code using `codeunits` for `AbstractString`. Most implementations of `AbstractString` uses UTF8, such as `String`, `SubString{String}`, `StringView` (of StringViews.jl), the various types in InlineStrings.jl, and more. But this is not generally true.

I propose to include a trait function `encoding(::Type{<:AbstractString})::Symbol`. In Base, the default implementations should be:
```julia
encoding(::Type{<:AbstractString}) = :unknown
encoding(::Type{String}) = :utf8
encoding(::Type{<:SubString{T}}) where T = encoding(T)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52704/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52704/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1877754583","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52704#issuecomment-1877754583","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52704","id":1877754583,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5v7ELX","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-01-04T21:01:07Z","updated_at":"2024-01-04T21:01:07Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Bikeshedding a bit - If `String` can be "UTF-8" without being valid UTF-8, then I feel like `encoding` is a little strong as a name. intrinisic/function/macro like assume ","user":{"login":"gbaraldi","id":28694980,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4Njk0OTgw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/28694980?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi","html_url":"https://github.com/gbaraldi","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gbaraldi/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-01-10T19:31:29Z","updated_at":"2024-01-11T00:29:37Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"One thing that is sometimes useful when writing very optimized code is to tell the compiler that `x` thing may not happen (basically declaring the behaviour of the function undefined if it doesn't meet the condition), which allows for extra optimizations to happen. We have a version of this in `@assume_effects`, but as the discussion in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/52828 showed, there are some places where we can't get around it.

For a syntax proposal I imagine something similar to the new C++23 assume might be interesting, which for us probably looks like
```julia
function foo(x)
	@assume x > 0
	sqrt(x)
end
```
Which would elide the check in foo. 
This made me think of the effect preconditions discussion that @Keno proposed a while ago, which would have effects conditional on something, and I guess `@assume` would be the manual override.

For reference these are the assumes that LLVM has https://llvm.org/docs/LangRef.html#int-assume and https://llvm.org/docs/LangRef.html#assume-opbundles","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52851/reactions","total_count":3,"+1":3,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52851/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1885981480","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52851#issuecomment-1885981480","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52851","id":1885981480,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5wacso","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-01-11T00:29:35Z","updated_at":"2024-01-11T00:29:35Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"`@assume_effects` applies to the contained block, so maybe `@assume` should be

```jl
function f()
  @assume x > 0 begin
    sqrt(x)
  end
end
```
it's also useful that you can visually see you're in a special block rather than having to read all the previous lines to notice the `@assume`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1885981480/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

julia> Pkg.add("Cthulhu")
   Resolving package versions...
```

In https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3757 I forgot I had set `JULIA_PKG_PRESERVE_TIERED_INSTALLED` and I didn't notice I was using `tiered_installed`. It would be useful to mention in the `add` output which strategy is being used

```
Resolving package versions using strategy: tiered_installed (JULIA_PKG_PRESERVE_TIERED_INSTALLED is set)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3758/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3758/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

documentation building system"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-01-04T19:52:26Z","updated_at":"2024-01-14T23:44:49Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"```
help?> Docs.doc
  Docs.doc(binding, sig)


  Return all documentation that matches both binding and sig.

  If getdoc returns a non-nothing result on the value of the binding, then a dynamic
  docstring is returned instead of one based on the binding itself.
```

It's useful to have a way to get the doc of a symbol in a module. `doc` is exported but can't be called because the first argument is a `Docs.Binding` which isn't public. 

Imho the public function should have been `doc(module, symbol)`, not `doc(binding, sig)`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52747/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52747/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1891114175","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52747#issuecomment-1891114175","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52747","id":1891114175,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5wuBy_","user":{"login":"jariji","id":96840304,"node_id":"U_kgDOBcWqcA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/96840304?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji","html_url":"https://github.com/jariji","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jariji/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-01-14T23:44:22Z","updated_at":"2024-01-14T23:44:22Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Can __safe_getindex(@nospecialize(t::Tuple), i::Int) = (@_nothrow_noub_meta; getfield(t, i, false))
```
which calls

```
# can be used in place of `@assume_effects :nothrow :noub` (supposed to be used for bootstrapping)
macro _nothrow_noub_meta()
```

so I wonder if `@assume_effects :nothrow :noub` would work here?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1894711061/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

It'd be nice to have an API that does

```julia
f(::Val{x}) where x = x
```

If it already exists, it'd be nice to mention it in `Val`'s docstring.

If it does not exist, maybe `getindex` or `get` would be a nice function for this? Or maybe add a new function?

This came up in https://github.com/JuliaArrays/StaticArrays.jl/pull/702#discussion_r379254870
